Calculation.Text: Substitute ( 10^Order - 1 ; 9 ; " " ) //This calculation can be a bit hard to figure out unless you hand calculate it a step at a time. //10^Order is an exponent calculation, 10 rased to the power of the value of Order. If order = 3, this part evaluates as 10^3 or 1000 //Subtracting 1 changes the value to a number where all the digits are 9's. If order = 3, you get 1000- 1 or 999. Note that the number of digits = the value of Order //The substitute function then replaces each digit of 9 with a space so 999 becomes " " or 3 space characters. The number of spaces equals the value of Order //Since spaces are not visible, they do not appear in a value list when you use this field as Field 2 in a value list and specify field 2 as the sort field.
